_comment: Traversal and staleness check intervals for Creator OS source intelligence. Applies to both tools/traversal_engine.py and tools/source_currency.py. Edit freely — changes take effect immediately on the next tool run.

seo-authority._note: Google and YouTube algorithm signals move fast. Weekly is the right default.

platform-spec._note: Platform specs change less often than algorithm signals. Bi-weekly is sufficient.

api-changelog._note: API changes can break integrations quickly. Bi-weekly check keeps the system current.

rate-benchmark._note: Rate reports are published annually or semi-annually. Quarterly check is sufficient.

tool-mcp._note: MCP and tool updates can affect skill compatibility. Bi-weekly is prudent.

partner-site._note: Partner site changes (new campaigns, removed pages) are operationally important. Weekly.

niche-authority._note: Editorial sites update regularly but rarely break skills. Monthly traversal finds new reference content.

competitor-page._note: Competitor video and channel pages. Snapshot saved offline via competitor_snapshot.py for ytInitialPlayerResponse extraction. 3-day check interval keeps tag/hashtag intelligence fresh. traversal_depth=0 — we do not follow links from competitor pages.

legal-authority._note: FTC endorsement guides, copyright basics, contract references. Change semi-annually. Without this override the traversal engine treated legal sources as 7-day-due (the global default), contradicting their intent.

cost-vendor._note: Materials-pricing vendor pages for the cost library. Quarterly is sufficient; prices drift but the cost tool null-and-flags rather than trusting a stale scrape.

software-dependency._note: pip packages and system binaries. NOT web-traversed (traverse-all skips this category). Authoritative freshness is computed per-package by tools/dependency_currency.py against PyPI / GitHub Releases from each entry's own check_interval_days; this override only governs the generic source_currency report grouping.

mcp-server._note: Third-party MCP servers (stats fleet, Google/Microsoft). NOT web-traversed. Version drift checked by tools/dependency_currency.py against their GitHub release pages; a tool-name or auth change silently drops a connector.

_traversal_depth_note: Never exceed depth 2 automatically. Depth 3+ requires explicit --depth=3 override and human review of all candidates before accept.
_allowlist_note: Only outlinks from pages on this list are followed during traversal. To add a domain, append it here. Removing a domain does not prune existing registry entries.
_note: To change any interval, edit this file. Use tools/traversal_engine.py --set-interval --category=<cat> <days> as a convenience shortcut, or edit the JSON directly.
